examples.title: Algorithm: MIMD Parallel Processing Model
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating how multiple processors execute independent instructions on different data in an MIMD system
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MIMD_Parallel_Processing
INPUT: Array of tasks T[0..n-1], Number of processors P[0..m-1]
OUTPUT: Combined results from all processors

BEGIN
 // Distribute tasks among processors
 FOR each processor p_i IN P DO
 START p_i
 // Each processor independently gets its subset of tasks
 tasks_i = DISTRIBUTE(T, p_i)
 
 // Each processor executes different instructions on its data
 FOR each task t IN tasks_i DO
 result_i = EXECUTE_INDEPENDENT(task t)
 STORE result_i IN local_memory[p_i]
 END FOR
 
 // Asynchronous execution - no synchronization needed here
 // Processors work at their own pace
 END FOR
 
 // Synchronize at barrier when needed
 BARRIER_SYNCHRONIZE()
 
 // Gather results from all processors
 final_results = GATHER(local_memory, all processors)
 
 RETURN final_results
END

// Algorithm for Shared Memory MIMD
ALGORITHM Shared_Memory_MIMD_Update
INPUT: Shared array data[], processor id pid, new value
OUTPUT: Updated shared data

BEGIN
 // Each processor can read/write shared memory independently
 LOCK(shared_memory_access) // Only for critical sections
 data[pid] = new_value
 // Other processors will see this update (cache coherence)
 UNLOCK(shared_memory_access)
END

// Algorithm for Distributed Memory MIMD
ALGORITHM Distributed_Memory_MIMD_Communicate
INPUT: Local data, destination processor, message
OUTPUT: Sent message to destination

BEGIN
 // Explicit message passing required
 SEND(message, TO destination_processor)
 RECEIVE(message, FROM source_processor)
 // Data must be explicitly communicated
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the core MIMD concept: each processor executes different instructions on different data independently. The shared memory version shows implicit communication through memory, while distributed memory shows explicit message passing required in distributed MIMD systems.

examples.title: Python: MIMD System Simulation with Message Passing
examples.description: Python simulation demonstrating distributed memory MIMD processing with message passing between independent processors
examples.code:
"""
MIMD Distributed Memory System Simulation
Demonstrates independent processors communicating via message passing
"""
import threading
import queue
import time
import random

class Processor:
 """Represents a processor in a distributed MIMD system"""
 
 def __init__(self, processor_id, message_queues):
 self.processor_id = processor_id
 self.local_memory = {} # Private local memory
 self.message_queues = message_queues # Communication channels
 self.running = True
 
 def send_message(self, dest_id, message):
 """Send message to another processor (non-blocking)"""
 self.message_queues[dest_id].put({
 'sender': self.processor_id,
 'data': message,
 'timestamp': time.time()
 })
 print(f"P{self.processor_id} -> P{dest_id}: {message}")
 
 def receive_message(self, timeout=0.1):
 """Receive message from another processor"""
 try:
 msg = self.message_queues[self.processor_id].get(timeout=timeout)
 return msg
 except queue.Empty:
 return None
 
 def process_task(self, task_data):
 """Process a task independently - different instructions on different data"""
 # Each processor can execute different logic
 result = task_data * 2 if self.processor_id % 2 == 0 else task_data ** 2
 self.local_memory['last_result'] = result
 return result
 
 def run(self, tasks):
 """Main processing loop for this processor"""
 print(f"Processor {self.processor_id} started with {len(tasks)} tasks")
 
 for task in tasks:
 # Independent execution - each processor works on its own data
 result = self.process_task(task)
 print(f"P{self.processor_id}: processed {task} -> {result}")
 
 # Simulate some computation time
 time.sleep(random.uniform(0.1, 0.3))
 
 print(f"Processor {self.processor_id} finished. Local memory: {self.local_memory}")


def simulate_mimd_distributed():
 """Simulate a distributed memory MIMD system"""
 num_processors = 4
 message_queues = {i: queue.Queue() for i in range(num_processors)}
 
 # Create processors
 processors = [Processor(i, message_queues) for i in range(num_processors)]
 
 # Distribute tasks - each processor gets different tasks
 all_tasks = list(range(20))
 tasks_per_processor = [
 all_tasks[i::num_processors] for i in range(num_processors)
 ]
 
 # Start all processors (independent execution)
 threads = []
 for i, proc in enumerate(processors):
 t = threading.Thread(target=proc.run, args=(tasks_per_processor[i],))
 threads.append(t)
 t.start()
 
 # Wait for all to complete
 for t in threads:
 t.join()
 
 print("\n=== All processors completed ===")
 
 # Demonstrate message passing after processing
 processors[0].send_message(1, "RESULT: 42")
 processors[2].send_message(3, "DATA: [1,2,3]")
 

# Demonstrate cache coherence concept for shared memory MIMD
class SharedMemoryLocation:
 """Simulates a memory location with cache coherence (MESI-like)"""
 
 def __init__(self, value=0):
 self.value = value
 self.state = "I" # I=Invalid, S=Shared, E=Exclusive, M=Modified
 self.cache_owners = []
 
 def read_by(self, processor_id):
 """Processor reads this location"""
 if self.state == "M":
 # Write back before sharing
 print(f"Cache miss! P{processor_id} requests data in Modified state")
 self.value = self.value # Write back to memory
 self.state = "S"
 elif self.state == "E":
 self.state = "S"
 print(f"P{processor_id} reads: {self.value} (transition E->S)")
 else: # I or S
 print(f"P{processor_id} reads: {self.value} (state: {self.state})")
 return self.value
 
 def write_by(self, processor_id, new_value):
 """Processor writes to this location"""
 if self.state == "S" or self.state == "E":
 # Invalidate other copies
 print(f"P{processor_id} writes: {new_value} (Invalidating other copies)")
 self.state = "M"
 else: # I or M
 print(f"P{processor_id} writes: {new_value} (state: {self.state})")
 self.state = "M"
 self.value = new_value


if __name__ == "__main__":
 print("=== Distributed Memory MIMD Simulation ===\n")
 simulate_mimd_distributed()
 
 print("\n=== Cache Coherence (MESI) Simulation ===\n")
 shared_var = SharedMemoryLocation(100)
 shared_var.state = "E" # Start with Exclusive state
 
 shared_var.read_by(0) # P0 reads, stays shared
 shared_var.write_by(1, 200) # P1 writes, invalidates P0
 shared_var.read_by(0) # P0 must reload
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates two key MIMD concepts: 1) Distributed memory processing where each processor has its own local memory and communicates via explicit message passing, simulating MPI-like behavior. 2) Cache coherence mechanism (MESI protocol) showing how multiple processors maintain data consistency through invalidation when writing to shared locations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) for parallel processing where n=tasks, p=processors
examples.spaceComplexity: O(p + n) for processor memory and message queues

examples.title: C: OpenMP Shared Memory MIMD Processing
examples.description: OpenMP implementation demonstrating shared memory MIMD where multiple threads access shared global memory
examples.explanation: This OpenMP implementation demonstrates shared memory MIMD systems where multiple threads (representing processors) share a global address space. Key concepts shown: 1) Parallel for directive creates independent threads processing different data. 2) Different threads can execute different instructions (even/odd logic). 3) Shared variables demonstrate implicit communication through memory. 4) Critical sections handle cache coherence for synchronized updates. 5) Dynamic scheduling shows load balancing for optimal performance.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) where n=array size, p=number of threads
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for shared arrays + O(p) for thread stacks

examples.title: C: MPI Distributed Memory MIMD Processing
examples.description: MPI implementation demonstrating distributed memory MIMD where processors have private local memory and communicate via explicit message passing
examples.explanation: This MPI implementation demonstrates distributed memory MIMD systems where each process has its own private local memory. Key concepts: 1) Each processor allocates its own local arrays - no shared memory. 2) Explicit message passing via MPI_Send/MPI_Recv required for communication. 3) Different processors can execute different instructions (even/odd rank logic). 4) Collective operations (Gather, Reduce) aggregate results across processors. 5) No cache coherence needed since each process has private memory - this is more scalable than shared memory MIMD.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) for local processing + O(p * msg_size) for communication
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n/p) per processor for local arrays + O(n) for global result on root
